Chapter 1 Sales Data Analysis (2015–2020) 1.1 Learning Outcomes Analyse multi-region sales data Apply SUM, AVERAGE, and Growth formulas Create charts for comparison and trends Perform sorting and filtering 1.2 Dataset Region 2015 2016 2017 2018 2019 2020 North 42000 46800 51200 55600 60100 65400 South 35000 38500 41200 44800 48500 53200 East 28000 31200 35600 39400 43800 49100 West 32000 35800 40100 44200 49600 55300 1.2.1  Download Excel File Download Sales Data Excel 1.3 Step 1: Data Entry & Formulas 1.3.1 Structure in Excel Column Description A Region B–G Sales (2015–2020) H Total I Average J Growth % 1.3.2 Formulas Used Column Formula Total (H4:H7) =SUM(B4:G4) Average (I4:I7) =AVERAGE(B4:G4) Growth % (J4:J7) =(G4-B4)/B4 1.3.3 Grand Total Row (Row 8) Calculation Formula Year-wise Total =SUM(B4:B7) etc. Overall Total =SUM(H4:H7) Avg of Avg =AVERAGE(I4:I7) 1.4 Step 2: Chart Creation 1.4.1 Recommended Charts Line Chart → Trend over years Column Chart → Region comparison 1.4.2 Diagram flowchart LR A[Select B3:G7] --> B[Insert Tab] B --> C[Line/Column Chart] 1.4.3 Insight North region has highest sales All regions show upward growth trend 1.5 Step 3: Chart Formatting Title: Regional Sales Trend (2015–2020) X-axis: Year Y-axis: Sales Legend: Regions 1.6 Step 4: Interpretation Sales across all regions show steady growth. North region leads consistently, while East shows gradual improvement. Overall performance indicates positive business expansion. 1.7 Step 5: Sorting 1.7.1 Example Sort by 2020 Sales → Identify top region Sort by Growth % → Identify fastest growing region 1.8 Step 6: Advanced Filter 1.8.1 Example Criteria Region North OR Growth % >0.5 1.9 Final Output Multi-region Excel analysis Charts showing trends and comparison Summary interpretation 1.10 Conclusion The Excel file demonstrates advanced data analysis including totals, averages, and growth percentage across regions, helping in business decision-making.
